metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Ravnica Remastered draws from nearly two decades of Ravnica cards across three blocks. Power level is moderate with notable rarity downshifts (Boros Elite, Thrill-Kill Assassin, Call of the Conclave, Glowspore Shaman). Most decks will be two-color with minimal splashing. Guild signets at uncommon enable more ambitious multicolor decks than typical, but the set does not encourage 5-color greed piles.
color_ranking: Strong guilds: Azorius, Dimir, Rakdos, Gruul, Orzhov

color_notes.Note: RVR is guild-driven. Stay 2-color unless a clear signal opens a multicolor splash. Track multicolor cards passed to read which guilds are open.

key_gameplay_notes: All 10 guild signets exist as fixing — multicolor is comfortable.
P1P1 rares/uncommons or top monocolor commons; stay open.
In 8-mans there are more guilds than players — read signals carefully.
Draft sideboard cards once you are full on playables.
5-color Gates lacks payoffs (only Gate Colossus); do not force it.

mechanics.name: Populate
mechanics.summary: Create a token copy of a creature token you control. Core to GW Tokens.

mechanics.name: Evolve
mechanics.summary: Put a +1/+1 counter on this creature when a larger creature enters.

mechanics.name: Extort
mechanics.summary: Whenever you cast a spell, opponents lose 1 and you gain 1.

mechanics.name: Afterlife
mechanics.summary: Creates 1/1 flying spirit tokens when the creature dies.

mechanics.name: Battalion
mechanics.summary: Triggers when three or more creatures attack together.

mechanics.name: Unleash
mechanics.summary: Creature enters with +1/+1 counter and can't block.

mechanics.name: Mentor
mechanics.summary: Put a +1/+1 counter on target creature with lesser power.

mechanics.name: Dredge
mechanics.summary: Discard to mill cards from your library to graveyard.

tier_1.name: Boros Aggro
tier_1.strategy: Aggressive shell with the best multicolor cards in the set. Haste creatures and battalion payoffs.

tier_1.name: Izzet Spells
tier_1.strategy: Pair creature payoffs with instants/sorceries. Curve out and finish with reach.

tier_1.name: Selesnya Tokens
tier_1.strategy: Synergistic plan using tokens for convoke and populate. Otherwise marginal commons like Siege Wurm and Conclave Equenaut shine here.

tier_2.name: Azorius Tempo
tier_2.strategy: Fliers and tempo plays with value generation. Hold the ground with fat blockers.

tier_2.name: Dimir Control
tier_2.strategy: Straightforward control combining blue card advantage with black removal. Bombs + removal + card advantage.

tier_2.name: Rakdos Aggro
tier_2.strategy: Aggressive with a sacrifice subtheme. Pair fodder (Scorched Rusalka, Dreadmalkin) with payoffs (Mayhem Devil). Reach via burn.

tier_2.name: Gruul Stompy/Ramp
tier_2.strategy: Lean aggro or bigger ramp approach. Aggressive builds are preferred over pure ramp.

tier_2.name: Orzhov Extort / Grind
tier_2.strategy: Grindy deck dealing incremental damage through extort. Alternative sacrifice approach with afterlife and Cartel Aristocrat.

tier_3.name: Golgari Graveyard
tier_3.strategy: Lacking dedicated payoffs despite the theme. Better to rely on individually powerful cards than synergy.

tier_3.name: Simic Ramp / +1/+1 Counters
tier_3.strategy: Either ramp/control or +1/+1 counter build. Struggles with removal — Band Together provides needed answer. Splash for removal/bombs.

gruul_bogles.description: Build around Silhana Ledgewalker with auras. Avoid edicts like Plaguecrafter and Undercity's Embrace.

five_color_gates.description: Minimal payoff — only Gate Colossus supports the archetype. Gates Ablaze, Guild Summit, and Gatebreaker Ram are absent. Do not force.

{W} White.name: Faith's Fetters
{W} White.note: Premium removal that answers most threats while padding life total.

{W} White.name: Boros Elite
{W} White.note: 3/3 for 1 mana; exceptional if supported, avoid otherwise.

{W} White.name: Summary Judgment
{W} White.note: Efficient removal missing vigilance creatures and some rares.

{U} Blue.name: Compulsive Research
{U} Blue.note: Strong card draw rewarding defensive play and 1-for-1 trading.

{U} Blue.name: Repeal
{U} Blue.note: Good tempo card, particularly efficient versus tokens.

{B} Black.name: Blade Juggler
{B} Black.note: One of Ravnica Allegiance's best commons.

{B} Black.name: Thrill-Kill Assassin
{B} Black.note: Split card flexibility; decide whether to unleash.

{B} Black.name: Ill-Gotten Inheritance
{B} Black.note: Provides reach for aggro or slow win-con for control.

{R} Red.name: Skewer the Critics
{R} Red.note: Mediocre when cast fairly, immense potential in aggro.

{R} Red.name: Rubblebelt Maaka
{R} Red.note: Combat trick option plus Hill Giant body.

{R} Red.name: Wojek Bodyguard
{R} Red.note: 3/3 for 3 plus mentor; pairs with Tin Street Dodger and Krenko's Command.

{G} Green.name: Band Together
{G} Green.note: Rare green removal using two creatures as instant answer.

{G} Green.name: Gather Courage
{G} Green.note: Free convoke trick with +2/+2 reach potential.

{G} Green.name: Crocanura
{G} Green.note: Defensive evolution creature; blocks all common fliers after evolution.

Multicolor.name: Blind Hunter
Multicolor.note: Exceptional rate for Orzhov at common.

Multicolor.name: Coiling Oracle
Multicolor.note: Simple value goodness; better with Keymaster Rogue.

Multicolor.name: Call of the Conclave
Multicolor.note: 3/3 token for two mana; synergizes with Rootborn Defenses and Eyes in the Skies.

Multicolor.name: Skyknight Legionnaire
Multicolor.note: Good aggressive flier with battalion synergy.

top_uncommons.name: Murmuring Mystic
top_uncommons.note: Best uncommon — better than many rares.

creatures.name: Karlov of the Ghost Council
creatures.note: Pairs with Ill-Gotten Inheritance and Mourning Thrull.

creatures.name: Niv-Mizzet, Parun
creatures.note: Possibly the most broken rare; requires UR.

general_tips: P1P1 rares/uncommons or top monocolor commons to stay open.
Track multicolor cards passed — they reveal which guilds are open.
Don't lock into a guild early unless a rare strongly demands it.
Prioritize monocolor staples for maximum flexibility.
Read the signal wheel to find underdrafted guilds.
In 8-mans there are more guilds than players; carefully evaluate signals.
Draft sideboard cards (Sprouting Renewal, Forced Landing, Crocanura, Mephitic Vapors) once full on playables.
